subject: Chemistry
q:
The organic compound that gives following qualitative analysis is:
  Test Inference
(a) Dil.HCl Insoluble
(b) NaOH solution Soluble
(c) Br2/water Decolourization
question:
The organic compound that gives following qualitative analysis is:
  Test Inference
(a) Dil.HCl Insoluble
(b) NaOH solution Soluble
(c) Br2/water Decolourization
solution:
(i) Aromatic compounds having +M group can decolourise bromine water.
(ii) Since the compound is insoluble in HCl, it shows that the compound is acidic.
(iii) As the compound is soluble in NaOH, this again shows that the compound is acidic.
The above three conclusions indicate that the compound is aromatic having +M group and acidic.
Hence, the answer is a phenol as it is aromatic and slightly acidic.

q: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>MgBr</mi></math> on treatment with methanol produces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of a gas at STP. The weight of gas produced is____mg (nearest integer)
question: A <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>MgBr</mi></math> on treatment with methanol produces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of a gas at STP. The weight of gas produced is____mg (nearest integer)
solution:
In the above reaction the Ethyl group of grignard reagent acts as a strong base.

As per the stoichiometric equation the number of moles of methanol is equal to the number of moles of ethane.

Volume of methanol=Volume of ethane=2.24ml

Hence, number of moles of ethane

n=2.24×10-322.4=10-4

Weight of the gas= Number of moles× Molecular mass

W=n×M

=10-4×30=3mg

subject: Chemistry
q: $4.7$ g of phenol is heated with Zn to give product X. If this reaction goes to $60\%$ completion then the number of moles of compound X formed will be _______ $\times 10^{-2}$. (Nearest Integer) (Given molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$: H:1, C:12, O:16)
question: $4.7$ g of phenol is heated with Zn to give product X. If this reaction goes to $60\%$ completion then the number of moles of compound X formed will be _______ $\times 10^{-2}$. (Nearest Integer) (Given molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$: H:1, C:12, O:16)
solution:
The chemical reaction of phenol with zinc dust is given by:

$C_6H_5OH + Zn \xrightarrow{\Delta} C_6H_6 + ZnO$

Product X is benzene ($C_6H_6$).

Molar mass of phenol ($C_6H_5OH$) = $6 \times 12 + 6 \times 1 + 16 = 94$ g mol$^{-1}$

Number of moles of phenol taken = $\dfrac{4.7}{94} = 0.05$ mol

From the stoichiometry of the reaction, 1 mole of phenol produces 1 mole of benzene.

Theoretical moles of X formed = $0.05$ mol

Since the reaction goes to $60\%$ completion, the actual moles of X formed is:

$0.05 \times \dfrac{60}{100} = 0.03$ mol

This can be written as $3 \times 10^{-2}$ mol.

Answer: $3$

question: What will be the major product when m-cresol is reacted with propargyl bromide <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>≡</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> in presence of <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> in acetone?
solution: The m-cresol is the m-methyl phenol. Phenol is acidic in nature. It gives the acid-base reaction with base. So, when it reacts with the base, it will form the phenolate ion. After that phenolate ion act as a nucleophile. This nucleophile gives the nucleophilic substitution reaction with the alkyl halide. It will follow the SN2 reaction mechanism.

q: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
question: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Alcohols are weaker acids than water
Acid strength of alcohols decreases in the following $\mathrm{RCH}_2 \mathrm{OH}>\mathrm{R}_2 \mathrm{CHOH}>\mathrm{R}_3 \mathrm{COH}$
Carbon-oxygen bond length in methanol, $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}$ is shorter than that of $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length in phenol.
solution: The $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length in alcohols is $142 \mathrm{pm}$ and in Phenol it is $136 \mathrm{pm}$. The $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length in phenol is shorter than that in methanol due to the conjugation of unshared pair of electrons on oxygen with the ring, which imparts double bond character to the $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{O}$ bond.

question:

solution:

question: What is the structure of the major product when phenol is treated with bromine water?
solution: Phenol has activating (electron releasing) $-\mathrm{OH}$ group and bromine water supplies $\mathrm{Br}^{+}$ ion easily, hence under such conditions reaction does not stop at monobromo or dibromo stage but a fully brominated $(2,4,6$,-tribromophenol) compound is the final product.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Consider compounds A, B and C with following structural formulae
$A = $ CH$_3$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - OH
$B = $ CH$_2 =$ CH - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_3$
$C = $ HO - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH(OH) - CH$_3$
For the conversion of B from A, reagent (D) required is _________ and structural formula of product (E) obtained when C undergoes same reaction using excess reagent (D) is _________.
question:
Consider compounds A, B and C with following structural formulae
$A = $ CH$_3$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - OH
$B = $ CH$_2 =$ CH - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH$_3$
$C = $ HO - CH$_2$ - CH$_2$ - CH(OH) - CH$_3$
For the conversion of B from A, reagent (D) required is _________ and structural formula of product (E) obtained when C undergoes same reaction using excess reagent (D) is _________.
options:
DE
Conc. H$_2$SO$_4$CH$_2 =$ CH $-$ CH(OH)CH$_3$

DE
PCCHO $-$ CH$_2$ $-$ CH$_2$ $-$ CH $=$ CH$_2$

DE
PCCCH$_2 =$ CH $-$ CH $=$ CH$_2$

DE
Conc. H$_2$SO$_4$ or H$_3$PO$_4$CH$_2 =$ CH $-$ CH $=$ CH$_2$
solution:
Compound A is pentan-1-ol ($CH_3-CH_2-CH_2-CH_2-CH_2-OH$) and Compound B is pent-1-ene ($CH_2=CH-CH_2-CH_2-CH_3$).

The conversion of an alcohol to an alkene is a dehydration reaction, which requires an acidic dehydrating agent such as concentrated $H_2SO_4$ or $H_3PO_4$. Thus, reagent D is concentrated $H_2SO_4$ or $H_3PO_4$.

Compound C is butane-1,3-diol ($HO-CH_2-CH_2-CH(OH)-CH_3$).

When C is treated with excess dehydrating agent (reagent D), both hydroxyl groups undergo elimination (dehydration). The elimination of two water molecules yields a conjugated diene, which is highly stable due to resonance.

The product E formed is 1,3-butadiene ($CH_2=CH-CH=CH_2$).

Answer:
DE
Conc. H$_2$SO$_4$ or H$_3$PO$_4$CH$_2 =$ CH $-$ CH $=$ CH$_2$

(A) Phenol, Zn/∆ (I) Salicylaldehyde
(B) Phenol, CHCl3,NaOH,HCl (II) Salicylic acid
(C) Phenol, CO2,NaOH,HCl (III) Benzene
(D) Phenol,  Conc. HNO3 (IV) Picric acid

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
solution:
(A) When phenol is distilled with Zn-dust, it gives benzene. it is an example of reduction. Phenol loses one O atom.

(B) When phenol is treated with CHCl3 and NaOH followed by acidification, salicyaldehyde is formed. The reaction is known as Reimer Tiemann reaction.

(C) Reaction of phenol with sodium hydroxide and carbon dioxide is Kolbe's Schmidt reaction that is a electrophilic substitution reaction resulting in the formation of salicylic acid as shown in the reaction.

The major product obtained on interaction of phenol with sodium hydroxide and carbon dioxide is salicylic acid.

(D) With concentrated nitric acid, phenol is converted to 2, 4, 6-Trinitrophenol. The product is commonly known as picric acid.

subject: Chemistry
q: Arrange the following compounds according to increasing order of boiling points. $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_9\text{OH}$ (A), $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_9\text{NH}_2$ (B), $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10}$ (C) and $\text{C}_2\text{H}_5\text{NHC}_2\text{H}_5$ (D).
question: Arrange the following compounds according to increasing order of boiling points. $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_9\text{OH}$ (A), $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_9\text{NH}_2$ (B), $n\text{-}\text{C}_4\text{H}_{10}$ (C) and $\text{C}_2\text{H}_5\text{NHC}_2\text{H}_5$ (D).
solution:
Boiling point depends on the strength of intermolecular forces.

$n\text{-C}_4\text{H}_{10}$ (C) is a nonpolar alkane with only weak van der Waals forces, resulting in the lowest boiling point.

$n\text{-C}_4\text{H}_9\text{OH}$ (A) is an alcohol that forms strong intermolecular hydrogen bonds due to the high electronegativity of oxygen, giving it the highest boiling point.

Both $n\text{-C}_4\text{H}_9\text{NH}_2$ (B) and $\text{C}_2\text{H}_5\text{NHC}_2\text{H}_5$ (D) are amines and form hydrogen bonds, which are weaker than those in alcohols because nitrogen is less electronegative than oxygen.

Between the primary amine (B) and the secondary amine (D), the primary amine has two $\text{N-H}$ bonds available for hydrogen bonding and less steric hindrance, leading to stronger intermolecular forces and a higher boiling point than the secondary amine.

The increasing order of boiling points is $C < D < B < A$.

Answer: $C < D < B < A$

subject: Chemistry
Chemistry
q: A mixed ether $(\mathrm{P})$, when heated with excess of hot concentrated hydrogen iodide produces two different alkyl iodides which when treated with aq. NaOH give compounds $(\mathrm{Q})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$. Both $(\mathrm{Q})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$ give yellow precipitate with NaOI. Identify the mixed ether $(\mathrm{P})$ :
question: A mixed ether $(\mathrm{P})$, when heated with excess of hot concentrated hydrogen iodide produces two different alkyl iodides which when treated with aq. NaOH give compounds $(\mathrm{Q})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$. Both $(\mathrm{Q})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$ give yellow precipitate with NaOI. Identify the mixed ether $(\mathrm{P})$ :
solution:
The mixed ether (P) upon heating with conc. HI undergoes cleavage to form two different alkyl iodides.
Treatment with aq. NaOH converts these iodides to alcohols (Q) and (R).
Both (Q) and (R) giving yellow precipitate with NaOI indicates both are secondary or primary alcohols capable of iodoform reaction.
For both cleavage products to yield iodoform-positive alcohols, the mixed ether must have both alkyl groups capable of forming such alcohols.
Option (1) represents a symmetric mixed ether where both fragments upon HI cleavage yield alcohol products with methyl groups adjacent to OH (secondary alcohols) or methyl ketones after oxidation, both giving positive iodoform test.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The reactions which produce alcohol as the product are:
A. $\mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Mo}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}}$
B. $2 \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{CH}_{3}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COO}\right)_{2} \mathrm{Mn}}$
C. $\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\right)_{3} \mathrm{CH} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{KMnO}_{4}}$
D. $2 \mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{Cu} / 523 \mathrm{~K} / 100 \mathrm{~atm}.}$
E. $\mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}_{3} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{KMnO}_{4} / \mathrm{H}^{+}}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
The reactions which produce alcohol as the product are:
A. $\mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Mo}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}}$
B. $2 \mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{CH}_{3}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3} \mathrm{COO}\right)_{2} \mathrm{Mn}}$
C. $\left(\mathrm{CH}_{3}\right)_{3} \mathrm{CH} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{KMnO}_{4}}$
D. $2 \mathrm{CH}_{4}+\mathrm{O}_{2} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{Cu} / 523 \mathrm{~K} / 100 \mathrm{~atm}.}$
E. $\mathrm{CH}_{3}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}_{3} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{KMnO}_{4} / \mathrm{H}^{+}}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Analyzing each reaction for alcohol production:
Reaction A: $CH_4 + O_2 \xrightarrow{Mo_2O_3, \Delta}$ produces formaldehyde, not alcohol.
Reaction B: $2CH_3CH_3 + 3O_2 \xrightarrow{(CH_3COO)_2Mn, \Delta}$ produces acetic acid, not alcohol.
Reaction C: $(CH_3)_3CH \xrightarrow{KMnO_4}$ tert-butyl compound undergoes oxidation to produce tert-butyl alcohol as the primary product.
Reaction D: $2CH_4 + O_2 \xrightarrow{Cu/523K/100atm}$ produces methanol (CH₃OH), a primary alcohol.
Reaction E: The diene compound with $KMnO_4/H^+$ produces vicinal diol, which is technically an alcohol but not a simple monoalcohol.
The reactions that predominantly produce alcohols as main products are C and D.
